mapping.a_element: bosonization of charge degrees of freedom (separating charge and spin excitations)
mapping.b_element: approximation neglecting pion contributions (focusing on quark Green function dynamics)

mapping.a_element: energy scale separation (J << epsilon << E_F) with a critical threshold (J)
mapping.b_element: critical coupling constant (alpha_c = 0.43) separating subcritical and supercritical regimes

mapping.a_element: spectral function singularities near 3k_F (shadow band features)
mapping.b_element: qualitatively different behavior of the quark mass function above alpha_c (chiral symmetry breaking)

mapping.a_element: exact treatment of spin excitations alongside bosonized charge
mapping.b_element: non-perturbative treatment of the quark Green function in the infrared region

brief_justification: While both papers address critical thresholds in strongly interacting systems (spin exchange coupling J vs. coupling constant alpha_c) and separate degrees of freedom (charge/spin vs. quark/pion), their underlying machinery differs: Paper A uses bosonization and Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid theory, while Paper B relies on Gribov's equation for the quark Green function in QCD. The mathematical structures (bosonization vs. integral equations for propagators) are distinct.
